lines.gurmukhi: ਜਬ ਉਸ ਕਉ ਕੋਈ ਦੇਵੈ ਮਾਨੁ ॥
lines.translit: jab us kau koee devai maan |
lines.english: When someone tries to appease her,
then she takes pride in herself.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਬ ਉਸ ਕਉ ਕੋਈ ਮਨਿ ਪਰਹਰੈ ॥
lines.translit: jab us kau koee man paraharai |
lines.english: But when someone puts her out of his thoughts,
She seems to please, but in the end, she deceives.
then she serves him like a slave. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਇਕਤੁ ਠਉਰ ਓਹ ਕਹੀ ਨ ਸਮਾਵੈ ॥
lines.translit: eikat tthaur oeh kahee na samaavai |
lines.english: She does not remain in any one place.
The Lord's humble servants cut her apart into pieces. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਇਸੁ ਸੰਗਿ ਰਾਚੈ ਸੁ ਕਛੂ ਨ ਲਹੈ ॥
lines.translit: eis sang raachai su kachhoo na lahai |
lines.english: Whoever is infatuated with her obtains nothing.
Whoever begs from her remains hungry.
She has bewitched a great many worlds.
But one who renounces her, and joins the Society of the Saints,
Raamkalee, Fifth Mehl:
by great good fortune, O Nanak, is saved. ||4||18||29||
Know that the priceless jewel is within your own heart.
See the Lord, the Universal Soul, in all.
The One God is perfect, and all-pervading.
Drink in the Ambrosial Nectar, by the Grace of the Saints.
Realize that your essence is within your own self. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਵਡੇ ਭਾਗ ਹੋਵਹਿ ਤਉ ਪਾਈਐ ਬਿਨੁ ਜਿਹਵਾ ਕਿਆ ਜਾਣੈ ਸੁਆਦੁ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: vadde bhaag hoveh tau paaeeai bin jihavaa kiaa jaanai suaad |1| rahaau |
lines.english: One who is blessed with high destiny, obtains it. Without a tongue, how can one know the taste? ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਠ ਦਸ ਬੇਦ ਸੁਨੇ ਕਹ ਡੋਰਾ ॥
lines.translit: atth das bed sune keh ddoraa |
lines.english: How can a deaf person listen to the eighteen Puraanas and the Vedas?
The blind man cannot see even a million lights.
The beast loves grass, and remains attached to it.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਸੁ ਨਹੀ ਬੁਝਾਵੈ ਸੋ ਕਿਤੁ ਬਿਧਿ ਬੁਝੈ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: jis nahee bujhaavai so kit bidh bujhai |2|
lines.english: One who has not been taught - how can he understand? ||2||
God, the Knower, knows all.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਤਿਨ ਜਮ ਨੇੜਿ ਨ ਆਵਹਿ ॥੩॥੧੯॥੩੦॥
lines.translit: naanak tin jam nerr na aaveh |3|19|30|
lines.english: O Nanak - the Messenger of Death does not even approach them. ||3||19||30||
He is with His devotees, through and through.
Those who sing God's Praises with joy and delight,

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਧਨੁ ਰਾਸਿ ਨਿਰਾਸ ਇਹ ਬਿਤੁ ॥
lines.translit: har dhan raas niraas ih bit |
lines.english: The Lord's wealth is my capital. False hope has left me; this is my wealth.
Raamkalee, Fifth Mehl:
Blessing me with His Name, He has purified and sanctified me.
The unstruck sound current vibrates and resounds.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਹਰਿ ਭਗਤਿ ਰਾਮ ਗੁਣ ਗਾਏ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: har har bhagat raam gun gaae |1|
lines.english: I am a devotee of the Lord, Har, Har; I sing the Glorious Praises of the Lord. ||1||
Breaking my bonds, the Lord has linked me to His service.

lines.gurmukhi: ਰਸਕਿ ਰਸਕਿ ਗੁਣ ਗਾਵਹਿ ਹਰਿ ਜਨ ਅਪਨੈ ਗੁਰਦੇਵਿ ਨਿਵਾਜਾ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: rasak rasak gun gaaveh har jan apanai guradev nivaajaa |1| rahaau |
lines.english: The Lord's humble servants sing His Glorious Praises with love and delight; they are honored by the Divine Guru. ||1||Pause||
My pre-ordained destiny has been activated;
I have awakened from the sleep of countless incarnations.
In the Saadh Sangat, the Company of the Holy, my aversion is gone.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਨੁ ਤਨੁ ਰਾਤੋ ਹਰਿ ਕੈ ਰੰਗਿ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: man tan raato har kai rang |2|
lines.english: My mind and body are imbued with love for the Lord. ||2||
He lifted me up and pulled me out, when I was suffering in pain. ||3||
In His Mercy, God has taken pity on me;
The Merciful Savior Lord has saved me.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾ ਕਿਛੁ ਸੇਵਾ ਨਾ ਕਿਛੁ ਘਾਲ ॥
lines.translit: naa kichh sevaa naa kichh ghaal |
lines.english: I have no service or work to my credit.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੁਣਿ ਸੁਣਿ ਉਪਜਿਓ ਮਨ ਮਹਿ ਚਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: sun sun upajio man meh chaau |
lines.english: Listening, listening to His Praises, joy has welled up within my mind.

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਠ ਪਹਰ ਹਰਿ ਕੇ ਗੁਣ ਗਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: aatth pehar har ke gun gaau |
lines.english: Twenty-four hours a day, I sing the Glorious Praises of the Lord.
By Guru's Grace, Nanak is lovingly focused on the Lord. ||4||20||31||
Raamkalee, Fifth Mehl:
Singing, singing His Praises, I have obtained the supreme status.
He tries to get what he must give up.
Enticed by Maya, he takes the crooked path. ||1||
He collects those things which are worthless.
In exchange for a shell, he gives up a jewel.
You unfortunate man - have you no shame?

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੁਖ ਸਾਗਰ ਪੂਰਨ ਪਰਮੇਸਰੁ ਹਰਿ ਨ ਚੇਤਿਓ ਮਨ ਮਾਹੀ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: sukh saagar pooran paramesar har na chetio man maahee |1| rahaau |
lines.english: You do not remember in your mind the ocean of peace, the perfect Transcendent Lord God. ||1||Pause||
Such is your condition, you faithless cynic, which I have seen with my own eyes.
Nectar seems bitter to you, and poison is sweet.
You are fond of falsehood, fraud and egotism.
